architecture_fit.note: Single real attention head (one layer, no MLP) reading superposed e_A/e_B through the task's own W_Q·W_Kᵀ to compute A AND NOT B — minimal delta, genuine fit.

baseline_comparison.note: Both the framework linear baseline (chance) and a naive raw-direction ablation whose suppression collapses under superposition — a clean strawman-fails contrast.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built synthetic circuit (acknowledged); the naive-vs-corrected ablation is a real causal knockout of the load-bearing dual basis, but there is no trained model to patch.

operating_range.note: Holds flat across the full cos(theta) sweep 0.0–0.8 and records the rising ||x_A|| norm cost, but never pushes cos near 1.0 to exhibit actual metric breakdown.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set: x_A solved as the dual (Gram-inverse) basis through the real QK matrix — exactly the hand-written-circuit case.

visual_judgement.note: Clean 3-panel Demo (suppression gap vs cos, norm cost, attention bars at hard cos); app boots cleanly, though bars show only the hardest slice.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies continuous gap over lenient AUC, the norm-cost price of superposition, and the per-condition bars — each tied to the goal's question.

notes: An elegant hand-built single-head circuit that genuinely engages the superposition axis: features are written in superposition via e_A/e_B and read through the real QK matrix, with x_A solved as the dual (Gram-inverse) basis so the NOT logit is exact across the whole sweep (robustness 1.0). It carries both the framework baseline and a load-bearing naive ablation plus a norm-cost curve explaining why superposition is hard. Marked down only because the exact algebraic inverse means no on-model causal patching and no demonstrated breakdown near cos→1.
